In the conventional DDPM approach, where η is typically
set to 1, we deviate from the standard methodology by
adopting η = 0. This modification aligns our approach with
the deterministic accelerated generation process characteristic
of DDIM [26].
C. Universal T ransformer Framework for VPT Prediction
The diffusion model used for human trajectory prediction
exhibits certain efficacy [20]. However, the inclusion of the
implicit diffusion sample module, as adopted in this study to
mitigate data consumption, may inadvertently overlook local
correlations and fine-grained information. In this research,
we introduce a universal transformer framework [31], which,
unlike conventional transformers, offers the improved capabil-
ity to effectively address the precision deficit attributed to the
implicit diffusion sample module in the trajectory generation
process. The universal transformer uses self-attention across
all the input data in a sequence. Self-attention exclusively
relies on the input from the current time step to calculate
correlations with each temporal, thereby deriving the associ-
ated weights. As depicted in the decoder and prediction block
of Fig. 1, this inherent characteristic endows self-attention
with the capability to concurrently reason across multiple time
steps during trajectory prediction, without any dependency on
preceding time steps. At the given time step, denoted as t, the
input feature to the module is represented as Ht, comprising
three distinct tensors: query ( Q), key ( K ), and value ( V ). The
input may be represented in the following manner :
Q = Wq · Ht
K = Wk · Ht
V = Wv · Ht . (17)
Herein, the matrices {Wq , Wk, Wv} denote the convolution
weights used in this formulation. The process of similarity cal-
culation involves the computat
